@@ -0,0 +1,78 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "piraeus-datastore-647-resizing-of-lvm-after-host-reboot-not-working",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "piraeus-datastore: Resizing of LVM after Host-Reboot not working",
+    "description": "Resizing of LVM after Host-Reboot not working. Community-reported issue.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify piraeus-datastore troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your piraeus-datastore deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n piraeus-datastore -l app.kubernetes.io/name=piraeus-datastore
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=piraeus-datastore -n piraeus-datastore --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review piraeus-datastore configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant piraeus-datastore configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n piraeus-datastore -l app.kubernetes.io/name=piraeus-datastore
kubectl get configmap -n piraeus-datastore -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=piraeus-datastore
```
Hi,

I am using Linstor with Piraeus-Operator (2.5.0) on my Kubernetes cluster."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Resizing of LVM after Host-Reboot not working",
+        "description": ">  Why is this still not fixed in Piraeus-operator?

Because making LVM work consistently when running in a container is hard. Even harder if the solution should support Ubuntu, Debian, RHEL, Talos Linux, and whatever else might be running out there.
```yaml
09:41:28 root@k8s-w3:~> ll /dev/mapper/
total 0
drwxr-xr-x  2 root root      480 Apr  5 12:14 ./
drwxr-xr-x 24 root root     5.0K Apr  8 07:49 ../
crw-------  1 root root  10, 236 Mar 21 15:42 control
lrwxrwxrwx  1 root root        8 Mar 21 15:43 datavg-pvc--0926060d--020e--460d--9ad4--a38b903cc22f_00000 -> ../dm-20
lrwxrwxrwx  1 root root        8 Mar 21 15:43 datavg-pvc--11ec7e16--8679--477a--9d09--ec1f8ddfeec2_00000 -> ../dm-15
brw-rw----  1 root disk 253,   3 Apr  5 11:42 datavg-pvc--3335b921--6fc8--4b30--add0--83d4b504e40b_00000 
brw-rw----  1 root disk 253,  10 Apr  5 11:42 datavg-pvc--4
```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm Resizing of LVM after Host-Reboot not working is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=piraeus-datastore -n piraeus-datastore --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n piraeus-datastore --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": ">  Why is this still not fixed in Piraeus-operator?

Because making LVM work consistently when running in a container is hard. Even harder if the solution should support Ubuntu, Debian, RHEL, Talos Linux, and whatever else might be running out there.",

total 0

+        "---
apiVersion: piraeus.io/v1
kind: LinstorSatelliteConfiguration
metadata:
  name: udev
spec:
  podTemplate:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: linstor-satellite
        volumeMounts:
        - name: lvmconfig
          mountPath: /etc/lvm/lvm.conf
          subPath: lvm.conf
          readOnly: true
      volumes:
      - name: lvmconfig
        configMap:
          name: lvmconfig
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: ConfigMap
metadata:
  name: lvmconfig
data:
  lvm.conf: |
    activation {
      udev_sync=1
      monitoring=0
      udev_rules=1
    }
    devices {
      global_filter="r|^/dev/drbd|"
      obtain_device_list_from_udev=1
    }"
+      ]
+    }
